front: What is the science of life forms and living processes?
How many known species are there on Earth?

back: Standardisation of naming living organisms worldwide

front: What does identification involve in biology?
back: Correctly describing an organism and attaching the correct name to it

front: Who introduced the binomial nomenclature system?
Which code governs plant scientific names?
Which code governs animal scientific names?
What are the rules of biological nomenclature?
back: 1) Names in Latin and italics, 2) First word = genus, second = specific epithet, 3) Handwritten names are separately underlined

front: Who was Ernst Mayr and what did he pioneer?
back: Harvard evolutionary biologist called 'The Darwin of the 20th century' who pioneered the biological species definition. Won Balzan Prize (1983), International Prize for Biology (1994), Crafoord Prize (1999).

front: In what two ways are living organisms related?
back: Horizontally (same era/time period) and vertically (across generations/ancestry)
